title: Using a workflow to automate status changes between boards
excerpt: I have a Workflow set up between two boards, so when a sub-item is created in Board A an item is than created in Board B. Board B continues the workflow to have sub-items created dependent on the status (which is marked …

posts.author: kanderson8 (Kari A.)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a Workflow set up between two boards, so when a sub-item is created in Board A an item is than created in Board B. Board B continues the workflow to have sub-items created dependent on the status (which is marked in Board B). I want the Workflow to continue so as the status of an item in Board B changes, it changes the status of the subitem in Board A. The changes may happen sporadically.

I have the workflow there, but I just can’t get it to work right.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about creating a workflow so that it ‘creates a new item’ AND uses a connected column to connect the two. Then use mirror columns on Board B, so that when the status changes, that it flows back?

image1007×759 46.4 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: kanderson8 (Kari A.)
posts.cookedHtml: That solved my problem of getting the lines connected when something new was created, so thank you very much for that! I figured out that you can set up an automation for a status to change based on the status of mirrored column, so I have brought that in. Overall my problem is solved, I just wish I didn’t have to have 4 different automations set up on Board A to change the status as the Board B Mirrored column is changed. Thank you!
